topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the university library hummed with an oppressive intensity, casting a sterile glare on the stack of papers spread across the mahogany table. My hands were clammy, the edges of the printed pages feeling brittle under my fingertips. Every time I glanced up, I saw the shadow of Professor Albright leaning against the doorway, his expression unreadable. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling at my diaphragm. I re-read the highlighted sections, trying to find a loophole in the phrasing, a way to reconstruct the ideas without copying. My mouth felt dry, refusing to cooperate as I tried to formulate a coherent defense, a shield against the accusation hanging heavy in the air.

[story 2]

"Are you certain about this, Alex?" Sarah whispered, her voice barely audible over the clatter of nearby keyboards. I swallowed hard, the movement catching in my throat. The accusation, delivered in the hushed tones of the department meeting, felt like a physical blow. My palms were slick against the worn cover of my notebook. I kept shifting in my seat, the cheap plastic chair squeaking in protest. I focused intently on the pattern of the wood grain on the desk, anything to avoid meeting anyone's gaze, anything to stop the churning sensation in my chest. The silence stretched, thick and suffocating.

[story 3]

I kept running the opening paragraph over in my head, my mind racing a thousand miles an hour, trying to build a narrative that would stand up to scrutiny. The air in the hallway felt thin, and my breath hitched with each step toward the administrative office. My fingers, gripping the strap of my backpack so tightly my knuckles were white, felt clumsy and useless. When the dean finally spoke, his voice was measured, and the weight of the entire situation settled squarely on my shoulders. I could feel a distinct pressure building behind my eyes, a desperate urge to simply disappear into the beige walls.
